mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of RSAFS over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a certain threshold signals a weakening labor market, leading to concerns about consumer spending and corporate profits, thus negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also rising, it indicates aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate valuations, negatively affecting S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 1 month
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS has increased by more than 0.25 percentage points in the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: In a strong economic expansion, robust housing starts (HOUST) above a certain level indicate healthy investment and consumer confidence, which translates into positive short-term market sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return during periods where HOUST exceeds 1500 units and the economy is in an expansionary phase is not statistically significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below a certain level signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, indicating weakening economic fundamentals that negatively impact S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of PAYEMS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also rising rapidly, it suggests the central bank is actively trying to cool an overheating economy, which can lead to a slowdown and negatively impact S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY > 3% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp in 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 YoY growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS has increased by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) below a certain threshold indicates an accommodative monetary policy, which supports economic growth and encourages investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2.0% is not statistically significantly positive.
